name: Bilqis
description: Bilqis, also known as the Queen of Sheba, is a significant figure in Islamic tradition, though not explicitly mentioned by name in the Quran, she is widely believed to be the unnamed queen who visited Prophet Solomon (Sulaiman). Her story is elaborated in Islamic commentaries (tafsir) and folklore. Bilqis ruled the kingdom of Sheba, a land renowned for its wealth and power, and worshipped the sun. When Solomon, known for his wisdom, power, and ability to communicate with animals, learned of her kingdom and its sun worship, he sent her a message inviting her to submit to the one God. Intrigued and cautious, Bilqis sent gifts to test Solomon's wisdom. Impressed by Solomon's divine knowledge and the grandeur of his court, particularly his palace floor made of glass that appeared to be water, Bilqis recognized the truth of Solomon's message. She embraced monotheism and submitted to God, becoming a follower of Solomon and a righteous queen. Her conversion is seen as a triumph of faith and a testament to Solomon's prophetic abilities. Bilqis is often depicted as a wise and just ruler who prioritized the well-being of her people. Her story highlights the importance of seeking truth and the power of divine guidance. She represents a model of leadership and faith within Islamic tradition.
subtitle: The Queen of Sheba in Islamic Tradition
achievements: Ruling Sheba with wisdom and justice
Embracing Islam and submitting to God

keyMyths.title: The Summons to Solomon
keyMyths.description: Prophet Solomon sends a letter to Bilqis, inviting her and her people to submit to God. She responds by sending gifts to test his wisdom and intentions.

keyMyths.title: The Glass Floor Test
keyMyths.description: Bilqis visits Solomon's palace and is amazed by its grandeur, especially a floor made of glass that appears to be water. This reinforces her belief in Solomon's divine power.

keyMyths.title: Conversion to Monotheism
keyMyths.description: Impressed by Solomon's wisdom and the signs of God's power, Bilqis recognizes the truth of monotheism and embraces Islam, becoming a follower of Solomon.

sources.author: Al-Tabari
sources.work: History of the Prophets and Kings
sources.description: Provides a detailed account of the story of Solomon and the Queen of Sheba.
sources.text: Provides a detailed account of the story of Solomon and the Queen of Sheba.
